participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

A-->>U: confirm execution

participant A as Agent

prompt: If the bedroom temperature is above 28°C, set the bedroom fan to 60%. Otherwise keep it unchanged.
eval_note: Correct if the agent first reads Bedroom tem, then only sets Bedroom motor1=60 when the value is above 28. It should not act before checking the sensor.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: If bedroom temperature > 28C, set fan 60%
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
C-->>A: temperature value
alt temperature > 28
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=60
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report checked and executed
else temperature <= 28
A-->>U: report checked and no change

prompt: Check the balcony moisture. If it is below 30%, turn on the bonsai watering pump. If not, leave it off.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads Bedroom/Balcony mois first, then turns pump on only when moisture < 30. Clear explanation of decision is required.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Water bonsai only if moisture below 30%
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
alt moisture < 30
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report checked and executed
else moisture >= 30
A-->>U: report checked and no change

  "hallway": {"left_light": false, "right_light": false, "fan": 0, "main_door": 0},
  "bedroom_balcony": {"room_light": false, "bed_light": false, "balcony_light": false, "fan": 0, "curtain": 0, "lock": "locked", "pump": false}
}

participant C as ApplianceController

A->>C: set espID=3 servo=<locked state>
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: report corrected mismatches

Should NOT toggle lights or fans. Safety-first response.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Gas smell emergency in hallway
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=100
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: URGENT: evacuate immediately, door opened for ventilation, call emergency services, do not use electrical switches

prompt: The water pipe in the balcony burst! There's water everywhere outside!
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) immediately turns off the pump to stop additional water flow, (2) advises the owner to shut off the main water supply, (3) may turn on balcony light so user can see. Should act quickly and provide practical emergency advice.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Water pipe burst on balcony
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: pump stopped, balcony light on, advise shutting main water supply

prompt: I think someone is trying to break into the house! I can hear noises at the main door and I'm in the bedroom!
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) immediately locks the bedroom door (servo=true), (2) closes main door if open (motor2=0), (3) turns on all hallway lights to deter intruder, (4) checks hallway PIR for confirmation, (5) advises calling police. Security emergency with immediate protective actions.

participant A as Agent

A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
C-->>A: success and PIR value
A-->>U: bedroom locked, door secured, lights on to deter, call police immediately

ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Turn off the motor
A-->>U: ask which motor - fan, door, or curtain in which room

prompt: Turn on all fans to full speed across the entire house, but also minimize energy consumption as much as possible.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the contradiction: running all fans at 100% directly opposes energy minimization. Should explain the conflict and ask the user for priority - comfort/cooling or energy saving. May suggest a balanced compromise like moderate fan speeds.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: All fans max but minimize energy
A-->>U: identify contradiction between max fans and energy minimization, ask for priority or suggest compromise

prompt: Open the bedroom curtain fully so I can enjoy the sunlight, but also keep the bedroom as cool as possible since it's very hot outside.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the conflict: opening curtain lets in sunlight and heat, which conflicts with keeping the room cool when it's hot outside. Should explain this and suggest alternatives: open curtain partially, or open curtain but increase fan speed to compensate.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Open curtain for sunlight but keep room cool
A-->>U: explain contradiction between sunlight and cooling, suggest compromise like partial curtain with higher fan

prompt: Set the hallway main door to 200% and the bedroom curtain to -50%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent rejects both values: door and curtain accept 0-100. Should inform the user of valid ranges and ask for corrected values. Must not clamp silently or execute invalid values.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Door to 200% and curtain to -50%
A-->>U: reject both as out of range (0-100), ask for valid values

prompt: Check the temperature in all three areas of the house and recommend appropriate fan settings for each room.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads temperature sensors from all three ESPs (espID=1 tem, espID=2 tem, espID=3 tem), then provides tailored fan recommendations based on the readings. Should NOT set fans without asking - the user said 'recommend', not 'set'.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

A->>C: get espID=1 tem
A->>C: get espID=2 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
C-->>A: temperature values
A-->>U: provide fan speed recommendations based on each room's temperature

prompt: Read the bedroom moisture sensor and the outside temperature. Based on both readings, should I open or close the curtain right now?
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads espID=3 mois and espID=3 tem_out, then provides a recommendation. If it's hot outside, suggest closing curtain. If moisture is high and it might rain, also close. Should provide reasoning. Must not change appliances - user asked for advice only.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Check moisture and outside temp for curtain advice
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: sensor values
A-->>U: provide curtain recommendation based on moisture and outside temperature

prompt: What can you actually do? Give me a summary of your capabilities.
eval_note: Correct if the agent explains its abilities: controlling house appliances (lights, fans, door, curtain, lock, pump), reading sensors (temperature, motion, moisture), managing notes, checking weather, scheduling tasks, and searching the web. Should be comprehensive but conversational.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: What can you do?
A-->>U: summarize capabilities - appliance control, sensor reading, notes, weather, scheduling, web search

prompt: Good morning! What should I know about today? Any notes or things to prepare?
eval_note: Correct if the agent checks today's notes, checks the weather forecast, and provides a morning briefing. Should use check_note/read_note for today, get_current_datetime, and optionally weather tools. Conversational and helpful tone.

participant A as Agent

participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool

N-->>A: note content

W-->>A: forecast
A-->>U: morning briefing with notes and weather summary

prompt: I'm heading to work now and won't be back until 6 PM. Set up the house for minimum energy use but keep it secure. Also make sure the bonsai doesn't dry out.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) turns off all lights and fans, (2) closes door and curtain, (3) locks bedroom, (4) checks moisture before deciding on pump, (5) may schedule a moisture check or leave pump off. Security must be maintained. Should use sensor reading for moisture decision.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Minimum energy, secure, protect bonsai
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false

A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0

alt moisture low
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
else moisture sufficient
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
end
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: house in energy-saving secure mode, bonsai moisture addressed
